modules.comments:
Re-exports all core Chart.js modules (types, config, FFI, callbacks,
colors) plus a pair of high-level convenience helpers (`createChartAuto`
and `updateChartAuto`) that automatically route through the overlay
merge path when the config contains non-JSON values.

declarations.comments:
Create a Chart.js instance with automatic overlay routing. Inspects
the config + callbacks via `hasOverlays`; if there are any non-JSON
values (callbacks, gradient/pattern colors, image point styles) it
dispatches to `createChartWithCallbacks`, otherwise it uses the plain
`createChart`. This is the safe default — it produces the same chart
either way and never silently drops an overlay value.

Consumers with no callbacks can pass `defaultCallbacks`:

```purescript
inst <- createChartAuto canvas myConfig defaultCallbacks
```
declarations.title: createChartAuto

declarations.comments:
Update a Chart.js instance with automatic overlay routing. Mirrors
`createChartAuto`: dispatches to `updateChartWithCallbacks` when the
config contains overlays, otherwise uses plain `updateChart`. The
`mode` parameter is forwarded to the underlying `chart.update(mode)`
call — pass `Just "none"` to suppress the animation on update.
declarations.title: updateChartAuto

modules.name: Chartjs

declarations.comments: Opaque type for a Chart.js tooltip item in tooltip callbacks.
declarations.title: TooltipItem

declarations.comments:
Tooltip formatting callbacks.
Callbacks receiving a single TooltipItem are called per-dataset.
Callbacks receiving an array are called once for the whole tooltip.
declarations.title: TooltipCallbacks

declarations.comments: Tick formatting callback: (value, index, ticks) -> formatted label
declarations.title: TickCallback

declarations.comments: Opaque type for a Chart.js legend item in legend callbacks.
declarations.title: LegendItem

declarations.comments: Opaque type for the Chart.js legend object in legend callbacks.
declarations.title: Legend

declarations.comments:
Input for the Halogen chart component: a config, optional callbacks,
and an update mode controlling animation on data changes.
declarations.title: ComponentInput

declarations.comments: Chart.js event callbacks (click, hover, resize, legend, tooltip, ticks).
declarations.title: Callbacks

declarations.comments: Opaque type for a Chart.js active element in event callbacks.
declarations.title: ActiveElement

declarations.comments:
Create a `ComponentInput` from just a `ChartConfig` (no callbacks).
Defaults to `Just "none"` for updateMode to suppress animation on updates.
declarations.title: simpleInput

declarations.comments:
Check if there are any overlays that need to be merged. Returns
`true` for any of: callbacks, non-CSS colors (gradient/pattern),
scriptable color functions (`SIFn`), image point styles, scriptable
non-color fields, per-chart-type dataset-defaults overlays under
`options.datasets`, or inline plugins. Drives the `createChartAuto`
routing decision.
declarations.title: hasOverlays

declarations.comments: Check if any callbacks are set.
declarations.title: hasCallbacks

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultTooltipCallbacks

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultCallbacks

declarations.comments: Convert Callbacks to a plain JS object for the FFI merge function.
declarations.title: callbacksToForeign

declarations.comments:
Build a combined overlays object containing callbacks plus dataset
overlays (non-CSS colors, scriptable color functions, image point
styles, scriptable non-color fields), plus per-chart-type dataset-
defaults overlays (same categories at `options.datasets.{type}`),
plus inline plugins (#24). This is the main entry point for the
FFI merge function.
declarations.title: buildOverlays

declarations.comments: A gradient color stop with an offset (0–1) and CSS color string.
declarations.title: ColorStop

declarations.comments:
Convert a `ScriptableIndexable PointStyle` to Foreign. `SIFn` functions
are wrapped as JS closures whose return value is passed through
`pointStyleToForeign` so the live Chart.js call site sees an already-
encoded point style (string or image reference).
declarations.title: scriptableIndexablePointStyleToForeign

declarations.comments:
Convert a `ScriptableIndexable Color` to Foreign. `SIVal` / `SIArray`
pass through `colorToForeign` directly; `SIFn` is wrapped as a JS
closure via `Chartjs.Scriptable.scriptableIndexableToForeign` so the
color returned by the PS function is `colorToForeign`-encoded before
Chart.js sees it.
declarations.title: scriptableIndexableColorToForeign

declarations.comments:
Convert a PointStyle to a Foreign value for direct JS use. String
variants become strings, `PSFalse` becomes the JS boolean `false`, and
`PSImage` passes through the wrapped `HTMLImageElement` /
`HTMLCanvasElement` reference unchanged.
declarations.title: pointStyleToForeign

declarations.comments:
Convert an `Indexable PointStyle` to Foreign. Retained for backwards
compatibility — new code should use `scriptableIndexablePointStyleToForeign`
since all PointStyle dataset fields are now `ScriptableIndexable` after
#22.
declarations.title: indexablePointStyleToForeign

declarations.comments:
Extract overlays from per-chart-type `DatasetsDefaults` entries
(`options.datasets.{line,bar,bubble,...}`). Returns `Nothing` when
every chart-type slot is either `Nothing` or contains only JSON-
compatible values. Otherwise returns a `Foreign` object keyed by
chart type string whose values are the per-type dataset overlays
from `extractDatasetOverlay` — the same shape `mergeOverlays` in
`FFI.js` already understands for regular dataset overlays, just
namespaced into a different merge target.

Without this helper, a consumer writing `SIFn`, a gradient, an
image point style on `DatasetDefaults` would
silently lose the value: the JSON encoder emits `null` for the
non-serializable form, and if nothing routes the real value onto
`config.options.datasets[type]`, Chart.js falls back to its built-
in palette.
declarations.title: extractDatasetsDefaultsOverlay

declarations.comments:
Extract dataset overlays for every dataset in the config. Covers the
same categories as `extractDatasetOverlay`: gradient/pattern colors,
scriptable color functions, and image point styles.
Returns `Nothing` if no dataset has any non-JSON values, signalling
the overlay merge can be skipped entirely.
declarations.title: extractDatasetOverlays

declarations.comments:
Extract non-JSON fields from a dataset (or dataset-defaults record)
as a Foreign object. Covers:

  * gradient/pattern colors and scriptable color functions (#21)
  * image point styles (#17) and scriptable pointStyle functions (#22)
  * per-segment line styling (#23, moved to JSON path in #32)
  * scriptable numeric / boolean / enum / compound fields (#22)
    — `SIFn` / `SFn` forms of borderWidth, borderRadius, border-
    Skipped, inflateAmount, base, pointRadius, radius, offset,
    rotation, borderAlign, circular, fill, etc.

Returns `Nothing` when every one of these fields contains only
JSON-compatible values.

Row-polymorphic in `r` so the same extractor works on both `Dataset`
(which adds `label` + `data` on top of `DatasetCommonRow ()`) and
`DatasetDefaults` (which closes the row with `()`). This is what
lets `extractDatasetsDefaultsOverlay` reuse the dataset logic without
duplication.
declarations.title: extractDatasetOverlay

declarations.comments: Create a radial gradient on a canvas.
declarations.title: createRadialGradient

declarations.comments: Create a pattern on a canvas from an image element.
declarations.title: createPattern

declarations.comments: Create a linear gradient on a canvas.
declarations.title: createLinearGradient

declarations.comments: Convert a Color to a Foreign value for direct JS use.
declarations.title: colorToForeign

declarations.comments: Convert a ChartConfig to the JSON structure Chart.js expects.
declarations.title: toChartJsConfig

declarations.comments: Opaque handle to a Chart.js instance.
declarations.title: ChartInstance

declarations.comments:
Update a chart with a new JSON config and a callbacks/overlays object.
The mode parameter controls animation: Nothing for default animation,
Just "none" to skip animation, or any other Chart.js update mode.
declarations.title: updateChartWithCallbacks

declarations.comments:
Update an existing chart with a new JSON config.
The mode parameter controls animation: Nothing for default animation,
Just "none" to skip animation, or any other Chart.js update mode.
declarations.title: updateChart

declarations.comments:
Toggle the visibility of a single data element by its index within
the dataset. Inverts the current visibility flag on the matching
meta entry. Call `updateChart` afterwards to render the change.
declarations.title: toggleDataVisibility

declarations.comments:
Export the chart as a base64-encoded image data URL.
The type parameter is a MIME type (e.g. "image/png", "image/jpeg").
The quality parameter is 0-1 for lossy formats like JPEG.
declarations.title: toBase64Image

declarations.comments: Stop the current animation. Returns the chart instance for chaining.
declarations.title: stopChart

declarations.comments:
Show a dataset or a specific data element with animation.
Pass Nothing for dataIndex to show the entire dataset.
declarations.title: showDataset

declarations.comments: Set the visibility of a dataset. Call `updateChart` afterward to render.
declarations.title: setDatasetVisibility

declarations.comments:
Programmatically set the active elements (e.g. to highlight a data
point from outside a hover handler). Each entry must specify a
`datasetIndex` and `index`. Call `updateChart` to apply.
declarations.title: setActiveElements

declarations.comments:
Adjust canvas dimensions. Pass `Nothing` for both width and height to
resize to match the container. Normally called automatically when the
container resizes; call this explicitly if you changed the container
size imperatively.
declarations.title: resizeChart

declarations.comments:
Restore the chart to its initial state before animations began. A
subsequent `updateChart` call will trigger new animations.
declarations.title: resetChart

declarations.comments:
Trigger a redraw of existing chart elements. Does NOT update elements
for new data — use `updateChart` when data changes.
declarations.title: renderChart

declarations.comments:
Check whether a Chart.js plugin is currently enabled by its plugin id
(the string returned by the plugin's `id` field). Useful for
conditional behavior in custom plugins.
declarations.title: isPluginEnabled

declarations.comments: Check whether a dataset is currently visible.
declarations.title: isDatasetVisible

declarations.comments:
Convert `InteractionOptions` to a plain JS object suitable for passing
to Chart.js's `getElementsAtEventForMode`. `Nothing` fields are dropped
so Chart.js uses its defaults for them. Exported for testing — callers
normally use `getElementsAtEventForMode` directly.
declarations.title: interactionOptionsToForeign

declarations.comments:
Hide a dataset or a specific data element with animation.
Pass Nothing for dataIndex to hide the entire dataset.
declarations.title: hideDataset

declarations.comments: Return the number of currently visible datasets.
declarations.title: getVisibleDatasetCount

declarations.comments:
Get the sorted list of visible dataset metadata objects. Each entry
is the same opaque `Foreign` shape returned by `getDatasetMeta`,
filtered to only the visible datasets and sorted in draw order.
declarations.title: getSortedVisibleDatasetMetas

declarations.comments:
Find chart elements matching an event for a given interaction mode.
Useful for click handlers that need to know which point/bar/arc was
hit. The `useFinalPosition` flag controls whether to use the
animation's final position (`true`) or current position (`false`).
declarations.title: getElementsAtEventForMode

declarations.comments:
Look up the internal metadata object for a dataset by index. The
returned `Foreign` is Chart.js's `ChartMeta` — a mutable bag of
controller/scale/data references. Consumers who need specific fields
(e.g. `data`, `visible`, `hidden`) coerce via `Foreign` operations.
declarations.title: getDatasetMeta

declarations.comments: Check whether a single data element is currently visible.
declarations.title: getDataVisibility

declarations.comments:
Look up an existing chart instance by its canvas element. Returns
`Nothing` if no chart is currently bound to the canvas. Useful for
accessing a chart from outside the code that created it (e.g. from
a separate event handler that has only the DOM reference).
declarations.title: getChartByElement

declarations.comments:
Look up an existing chart instance by canvas element id (the `id`
attribute of the underlying `<canvas>`). Returns `Nothing` if no
matching canvas exists or no chart is bound to it.
declarations.title: getChartByCanvasId

declarations.comments:
Get the array of currently active (hovered or otherwise highlighted)
chart elements. Each entry is an opaque `Foreign` wrapping Chart.js's
`ActiveElement` (which has `datasetIndex`, `index`, and `element`).
declarations.title: getActiveElements

declarations.comments: Destroy a chart instance and release its resources.
declarations.title: destroyChart

declarations.comments: Create a chart with a JSON config and a callbacks/overlays object.
declarations.title: createChartWithCallbacks

declarations.comments: Create a new Chart.js instance on a canvas element with a JSON config.
declarations.title: createChart

declarations.comments:
Clear the chart canvas. Used internally between animation frames;
rarely needed directly. Returns the chart instance for chaining.
declarations.title: clearChart

declarations.comments:
Reverse `chartRegister`. Removes the given components from the
global registry. Mostly useful for tests; production code rarely
needs to unregister.
declarations.title: chartUnregister

declarations.comments:
Get the global Chart.js registry as an opaque `Foreign` value.
The registry exposes typed sub-registries (`controllers`, `plugins`,
`scales`, `elements`) plus generic `add` / `remove` methods. Use
this for advanced registration scenarios — registering a custom
interaction mode (via `Chart.helpers.registry`) or a custom tooltip
positioner, both of which the typed PureScript API does not yet
model. Consumers using this should reference Chart.js's registry
API docs directly.
declarations.title: chartRegistry

declarations.comments:
Register one or more Chart.js components (controllers, plugins,
scales, or elements) globally. Each item must be a JS object the
Chart.js registry recognizes — typically a controller class with an
`id` field. After registration, the component is available to all
subsequent chart instances in this process. Pair with `CustomType`
from `Chartjs.Types` (#27) to use a registered controller as a
chart type.

The PS argument is `Array Foreign` because Chart.js components are
arbitrary JS objects (and registering plugins / controllers / scales
/ elements works the same way at the API level). Build the
components in JS or via `unsafeToForeign` from PS.
declarations.title: chartRegister

declarations.comments:
Encode a `Scriptable a` to `Foreign`. Static `SVal` values pass through
the supplied encoder; `SFn` functions become JS closures that apply the
encoder to their return value at call time — which is what Chart.js
expects when it resolves scriptable options at draw time.
declarations.title: scriptableToForeign
scriptableIndexableToForeign

declarations.comments: `true` when the value needs the overlay path because it holds a function.
declarations.title: hasScriptableIndexable

declarations.comments: `true` when the value needs the overlay path because it holds a function.
declarations.title: hasScriptable

declarations.comments:
Context passed by Chart.js to scriptable tooltip option functions.
Mirrors Chart.js's `ScriptableTooltipContext<TType>`. Different
from dataset-level `ScriptableContext` — has `tooltip` and
`tooltipItems` instead of `dataIndex`, `dataset`, etc. Both
`tooltip` and `tooltipItems` are left as `Foreign` because their
shape is complex (the full `TooltipModel` / `TooltipItem` types).
declarations.title: ScriptableTooltipContext

declarations.comments:
Chart.js's `ScriptableAndArray<T, TContext>` — a value that can be
a single value, a per-item array, or a scriptable function. Note
that Chart.js does **not** allow function entries inside the
array form, so this is a flat ADT rather than
`Indexable (Scriptable a)`.

Like `Scriptable`, the `SIFn` form cannot be JSON-serialized and
travels through the overlay path — the chart must be created via
`createChartAuto` (or `createChartWithCallbacks`) for an `SIFn`
value to take effect. `SIVal` / `SIArray` serialize as plain JSON
and work with the base `createChart` path.
declarations.title: ScriptableIndexable

declarations.comments:
Context passed by Chart.js to scriptable option functions at
resolution time. Mirrors Chart.js's `ScriptableContext<TType>`
interface. `chart`, `dataset`, `parsed`, and `raw` are left as
`Foreign` because their shape varies by chart type and parsing
config — consumers inspect them via `Foreign` operations.
declarations.title: ScriptableContext

declarations.comments:
A value that Chart.js accepts as either a static value or a
function computed from the resolution context. Chart.js calls
`Scriptable` functions once per affected element at draw time
to pick the concrete value. Use `SVal` for static values and
`SFn` (or the `scriptableFn` smart constructor) for dynamic ones.

`SFn`-wrapped functions travel through the overlay path: they
cannot be JSON-serialized, so the chart must be created via
`createChartWithCallbacks` or `createChartAuto` for the dynamic
form to take effect. `SVal`-only configurations serialize as
plain JSON and work with the base `createChart` path.
declarations.title: Scriptable

declarations.comments:
Scale `min` / `max` / `suggestedMin` / `suggestedMax` value. Numeric
for linear/logarithmic/radial scales, string for category scales
(matches a label) and time scales (an ISO date or any string the
configured time adapter parses). Chart.js's `CategoryScaleOptions`
and `TimeScaleOptions` both type these as `string | number`; the
base `CoreScaleOptions` types them as `unknown` (fully untyped).
declarations.title: ScaleMinMax

declarations.comments:
Outer radius for pie/doughnut charts. Same shape as `Cutout`: pixels or
percentage string (e.g. `"100%"`).
declarations.title: Radius

declarations.comments:
Point marker styles. `PSFalse` disables the marker. `PSImage` wraps an
`HTMLImageElement` or `HTMLCanvasElement` for image-based point styles —
the referenced element's natural `width` / `height` determine the drawn
size at render time (the `pointRadius` field does not apply).

Image point styles cannot be JSON-serialized and go through the overlay
mechanism in `Chartjs.Colors` / `Chartjs.Callbacks` — you must create the
chart via `createChartWithCallbacks` (or anything that builds overlays)
for a `PSImage` dataset pointStyle to render. Ensure the wrapped image
has finished loading before the chart is created; Chart.js reads
`width`/`height` at draw time and draws nothing if they are zero.

Note: `PSImage` is currently supported only on `Dataset.pointStyle`.
Setting it on options-level fields (legend, element defaults) silently
produces a `null` in the JSON config with no overlay replacement.
declarations.title: PointStyle

declarations.comments:
Custom parsing keys for mapping data object properties to axes.
Use `xAxisKey`/`yAxisKey` for cartesian charts, `key` for radial charts (pie/doughnut/radar).
declarations.title: ParsingConfig

declarations.comments:
CSS line-height value. Mirrors Chart.js's `FontSpec.lineHeight` which
accepts a unitless number or any CSS string (e.g. `"1.5em"`, `"150%"`).
declarations.title: LineHeight

declarations.comments:
Options passed to `getElementsAtEventForMode`. Matches Chart.js's
`InteractionOptions` interface — same fields as `InteractionConfig`
minus `mode` (which is passed as a separate argument to the method).
declarations.title: InteractionOptions

declarations.comments:
Single element returned from `getElementsAtEventForMode`. Matches
Chart.js's `InteractionItem`. `element` is the underlying Chart.js
element (point, bar, arc, etc.) — opaque, consumers inspect via
`Foreign` operations if needed.
declarations.title: InteractionItem

declarations.comments:
CSS font-weight value. Mirrors Chart.js's `FontSpec.weight` which
accepts specific keyword strings or a numeric weight (100–900).
declarations.title: FontWeight

declarations.comments:
Per-chart-type dataset defaults (`options.datasets` in Chart.js). Each
entry supplies default field values for datasets of that chart type.
declarations.title: DatasetsDefaults

declarations.comments:
Dataset defaults — same shape as `Dataset` minus the identity fields
(`label`, `data`). Used as the value type of per-chart-type entries in
`DatasetsDefaults` (`options.datasets.line`, `options.datasets.bar`, ...).
declarations.title: DatasetDefaults

declarations.comments:
Row of dataset configuration fields shared between instances (`Dataset`)
and per-type defaults (`DatasetDefaults`). Parameterised by `r` so the row
can be extended with identity fields (label/data) to form `Dataset`, or
closed (with `()`) to form `DatasetDefaults`.
declarations.title: DatasetCommonRow

declarations.comments: Unified dataset configuration covering all Chart.js chart types.
declarations.title: Dataset

declarations.comments:
Built-in `colors` plugin options. When `enabled` (default `true`), the
plugin auto-assigns colors to datasets from a built-in palette. Set
`forceOverride` to `true` to override any user-set colors.
declarations.title: ColorsPluginOptions

declarations.comments: A color value that Chart.js accepts: CSS string, gradient, or pattern.
declarations.title: Color

declarations.comments:
Supported Chart.js chart types. The 8 named constructors cover all
built-in types; `CustomType` is an escape hatch for chart types
registered at runtime via `Chart.register(MyController)`, where
`MyController` is a controller class (not a plugin) that defines a
new `id` such as `"financial"`, `"candlestick"`, or a custom
doughnut variant. The wrapped string in `CustomType` must match the
controller's `id` field so Chart.js can look it up at draw time.
Pairs naturally with roadmap item #25's registration FFI bindings,
but useful standalone for any consumer who has registered a
controller via raw JS.
declarations.title: ChartType

declarations.comments: Top-level chart options.
declarations.title: ChartOptions

declarations.comments: Complete chart configuration: type, data, and options.
declarations.title: ChartConfig

declarations.comments: Opaque type for a CanvasPattern object (from canvas 2D context).
declarations.title: CanvasPattern

declarations.comments: Opaque type for a CanvasGradient object (from canvas 2D context).
declarations.title: CanvasGradient

declarations.comments:
`ScriptableIndexable` field value wrapping a single static value
(`Just (SIVal a)`). Returns `Maybe` for record-literal ergonomics.
declarations.title: siValue

declarations.comments:
`ScriptableIndexable` field value wrapping a `ScriptableContext -> a`
function (`Just (SIFn f)`). Returns `Maybe` for record-literal ergonomics.
declarations.title: siFn

declarations.comments:
`ScriptableIndexable` field value wrapping a per-item array
(`Just (SIArray xs)`). Returns `Maybe` for record-literal ergonomics.
declarations.title: siArray

declarations.comments:
Wrap a static value as a `Scriptable` field value (`Just (SVal a)`).
Mirrors the `single` / `perItem` convention — returns `Maybe` so the
result can be dropped directly into a `Maybe (Scriptable a)` record
field without a manual `Just`. For pattern matching use the raw
`SVal` constructor instead.
declarations.title: scriptableValue

declarations.comments:
Wrap a `ScriptableContext -> a` function as a `Scriptable` field value
(`Just (SFn f)`). Returns `Maybe` for record-literal ergonomics.
declarations.title: scriptableFn

declarations.comments:
Convenience constructor for `PSImage` from a typed `HTMLImageElement`.
Equivalent to `PSImage (unsafeToForeign img)`. For `HTMLCanvasElement`
or other `CanvasImageSource` values, use `PSImage` directly with
`unsafeToForeign`.

The referenced image must be fully loaded before the chart is created —
Chart.js reads `width`/`height` at draw time and draws nothing if they
are zero. Route the config through `createChartWithCallbacks` so the
overlay merge can substitute the live reference into the config.
declarations.title: psImage

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultParsingConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultOptions

declarations.comments:
All optional fields set to `Nothing`. Default options for
`getElementsAtEventForMode` — Chart.js will use its built-in defaults
for any field left `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultInteractionOptions

declarations.comments: Default `DatasetsDefaults` with every per-type entry set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultDatasetsDefaults

declarations.comments:
Default `DatasetDefaults` with all optional fields set to `Nothing`. Use
as a starting point for per-chart-type defaults in `DatasetsDefaults`.
declarations.title: defaultDatasetDefaults

declarations.comments: Default dataset with empty label and data. All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultDataset

declarations.comments: Default chart config with `Bar` type, empty labels, and empty datasets.
declarations.title: defaultConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultColorsPluginOptions

declarations.comments: Convenience constructor for CSS color strings.
declarations.title: css

declarations.comments:
Convert to the Chart.js string representation. `CustomType` passes
the wrapped string through unchanged — Chart.js looks up the
corresponding registered controller at runtime.
declarations.title: chartTypeToString

modules.comments: Callback types and overlays for Chart.js event handling.

declarations.comments: Chart.js event callbacks (click, hover, resize, legend, tooltip, ticks).
declarations.title: Callbacks

declarations.comments:
Tooltip formatting callbacks.
Callbacks receiving a single TooltipItem are called per-dataset.
Callbacks receiving an array are called once for the whole tooltip.
declarations.title: TooltipCallbacks

declarations.comments: Tick formatting callback: (value, index, ticks) -> formatted label
declarations.title: TickCallback

declarations.comments: Scale lifecycle callback: receives the scale/axis object as Foreign.
declarations.title: ScaleCallback

declarations.comments:
Scale lifecycle callbacks for a single scale axis. These are set
directly on the scale config (not nested under ticks/grid).

The four high-value callbacks (`afterBuildTicks`, `beforeFit`,
`afterFit`, `afterDataLimits`) shipped with roadmap item #13. The
remaining 10 (the `before*` / `after*` pairs around update,
dimensions, data limits, build ticks, tick-to-label conversion,
and label rotation) shipped with #26 — Chart.js fires them at
every step of the scale's lifecycle, but in practice they're only
needed for advanced custom scales or debugging.
declarations.title: ScaleLifecycleCallbacks

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultScaleLifecycleCallbacks

declarations.comments: Opaque type for a Chart.js active element in event callbacks.
declarations.title: ActiveElement

declarations.comments: Opaque type for a Chart.js legend item in legend callbacks.
declarations.title: LegendItem

declarations.comments: Opaque type for the Chart.js legend object in legend callbacks.
declarations.title: Legend

declarations.comments: Opaque type for a Chart.js tooltip item in tooltip callbacks.
declarations.title: TooltipItem

declarations.comments:
Input for the Halogen chart component: a config, optional callbacks,
and an update mode controlling animation on data changes.
declarations.title: ComponentInput

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultCallbacks

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultTooltipCallbacks

declarations.comments:
Create a `ComponentInput` from just a `ChartConfig` (no callbacks).
Defaults to `Just "none"` for updateMode to suppress animation on updates.
declarations.title: simpleInput

declarations.comments: Convert Callbacks to a plain JS object for the FFI merge function.
declarations.title: callbacksToForeign

declarations.comments: Check if any callbacks are set.
declarations.title: hasCallbacks

declarations.comments:
Build a combined overlays object containing callbacks plus dataset
overlays (non-CSS colors, scriptable color functions, image point
styles, scriptable non-color fields), plus per-chart-type dataset-
defaults overlays (same categories at `options.datasets.{type}`),
plus inline plugins (#24). This is the main entry point for the
FFI merge function.
declarations.title: buildOverlays

declarations.comments:
Check if there are any overlays that need to be merged. Returns
`true` for any of: callbacks, non-CSS colors (gradient/pattern),
scriptable color functions (`SIFn`), image point styles, scriptable
non-color fields, per-chart-type dataset-defaults overlays under
`options.datasets`, or inline plugins. Drives the `createChartAuto`
routing decision.
declarations.title: hasOverlays

modules.name: Chartjs.Callbacks

modules.comments:
Gradient, pattern, color, and image-point-style utilities for
Chart.js datasets — everything that can't go through the plain JSON
serialization path and needs the overlay merge.

declarations.comments: A gradient color stop with an offset (0–1) and CSS color string.
declarations.title: ColorStop

declarations.comments: Create a linear gradient on a canvas.
declarations.title: createLinearGradient

declarations.comments: Create a radial gradient on a canvas.
declarations.title: createRadialGradient

declarations.comments: Create a pattern on a canvas from an image element.
declarations.title: createPattern

declarations.comments: Convert a Color to a Foreign value for direct JS use.
declarations.title: colorToForeign

declarations.comments:
Convert a `ScriptableIndexable Color` to Foreign. `SIVal` / `SIArray`
pass through `colorToForeign` directly; `SIFn` is wrapped as a JS
closure via `Chartjs.Scriptable.scriptableIndexableToForeign` so the
color returned by the PS function is `colorToForeign`-encoded before
Chart.js sees it.
declarations.title: scriptableIndexableColorToForeign

declarations.comments:
Convert a PointStyle to a Foreign value for direct JS use. String
variants become strings, `PSFalse` becomes the JS boolean `false`, and
`PSImage` passes through the wrapped `HTMLImageElement` /
`HTMLCanvasElement` reference unchanged.
declarations.title: pointStyleToForeign

declarations.comments:
Convert an `Indexable PointStyle` to Foreign. Retained for backwards
compatibility — new code should use `scriptableIndexablePointStyleToForeign`
since all PointStyle dataset fields are now `ScriptableIndexable` after
#22.
declarations.title: indexablePointStyleToForeign

declarations.comments:
Convert a `ScriptableIndexable PointStyle` to Foreign. `SIFn` functions
are wrapped as JS closures whose return value is passed through
`pointStyleToForeign` so the live Chart.js call site sees an already-
encoded point style (string or image reference).
declarations.title: scriptableIndexablePointStyleToForeign

declarations.comments:
Extract non-JSON fields from a dataset (or dataset-defaults record)
as a Foreign object. Covers:

  * gradient/pattern colors and scriptable color functions (#21)
  * image point styles (#17) and scriptable pointStyle functions (#22)
  * per-segment line styling (#23, moved to JSON path in #32)
  * scriptable numeric / boolean / enum / compound fields (#22)
    — `SIFn` / `SFn` forms of borderWidth, borderRadius, border-
    Skipped, inflateAmount, base, pointRadius, radius, offset,
    rotation, borderAlign, circular, fill, etc.

Returns `Nothing` when every one of these fields contains only
JSON-compatible values.

Row-polymorphic in `r` so the same extractor works on both `Dataset`
(which adds `label` + `data` on top of `DatasetCommonRow ()`) and
`DatasetDefaults` (which closes the row with `()`). This is what
lets `extractDatasetsDefaultsOverlay` reuse the dataset logic without
duplication.
declarations.title: extractDatasetOverlay

declarations.comments:
Extract dataset overlays for every dataset in the config. Covers the
same categories as `extractDatasetOverlay`: gradient/pattern colors,
scriptable color functions, and image point styles.
Returns `Nothing` if no dataset has any non-JSON values, signalling
the overlay merge can be skipped entirely.
declarations.title: extractDatasetOverlays

declarations.comments:
Extract overlays from per-chart-type `DatasetsDefaults` entries
(`options.datasets.{line,bar,bubble,...}`). Returns `Nothing` when
every chart-type slot is either `Nothing` or contains only JSON-
compatible values. Otherwise returns a `Foreign` object keyed by
chart type string whose values are the per-type dataset overlays
from `extractDatasetOverlay` — the same shape `mergeOverlays` in
`FFI.js` already understands for regular dataset overlays, just
namespaced into a different merge target.

Without this helper, a consumer writing `SIFn`, a gradient, an
image point style on `DatasetDefaults` would
silently lose the value: the JSON encoder emits `null` for the
non-serializable form, and if nothing routes the real value onto
`config.options.datasets[type]`, Chart.js falls back to its built-
in palette.
declarations.title: extractDatasetsDefaultsOverlay

modules.name: Chartjs.Colors

modules.comments: Converts PureScript chart configuration records to the JSON format Chart.js expects.

declarations.comments: Convert a ChartConfig to the JSON structure Chart.js expects.
declarations.title: toChartJsConfig

modules.name: Chartjs.Config

modules.comments: Low-level FFI bindings to Chart.js for creating, updating, and destroying chart instances.

declarations.comments: Opaque handle to a Chart.js instance.
declarations.title: ChartInstance

declarations.comments: Create a new Chart.js instance on a canvas element with a JSON config.
declarations.title: createChart

declarations.comments:
Update an existing chart with a new JSON config.
The mode parameter controls animation: Nothing for default animation,
Just "none" to skip animation, or any other Chart.js update mode.
declarations.title: updateChart

declarations.comments: Destroy a chart instance and release its resources.
declarations.title: destroyChart

declarations.comments: Create a chart with a JSON config and a callbacks/overlays object.
declarations.title: createChartWithCallbacks

declarations.comments:
Update a chart with a new JSON config and a callbacks/overlays object.
The mode parameter controls animation: Nothing for default animation,
Just "none" to skip animation, or any other Chart.js update mode.
declarations.title: updateChartWithCallbacks

declarations.comments: Stop the current animation. Returns the chart instance for chaining.
declarations.title: stopChart

declarations.comments:
Export the chart as a base64-encoded image data URL.
The type parameter is a MIME type (e.g. "image/png", "image/jpeg").
The quality parameter is 0-1 for lossy formats like JPEG.
declarations.title: toBase64Image

declarations.comments: Set the visibility of a dataset. Call `updateChart` afterward to render.
declarations.title: setDatasetVisibility

declarations.comments: Check whether a dataset is currently visible.
declarations.title: isDatasetVisible

declarations.comments:
Hide a dataset or a specific data element with animation.
Pass Nothing for dataIndex to hide the entire dataset.
declarations.title: hideDataset

declarations.comments:
Show a dataset or a specific data element with animation.
Pass Nothing for dataIndex to show the entire dataset.
declarations.title: showDataset

declarations.comments:
Trigger a redraw of existing chart elements. Does NOT update elements
for new data — use `updateChart` when data changes.
declarations.title: renderChart

declarations.comments:
Restore the chart to its initial state before animations began. A
subsequent `updateChart` call will trigger new animations.
declarations.title: resetChart

declarations.comments:
Adjust canvas dimensions. Pass `Nothing` for both width and height to
resize to match the container. Normally called automatically when the
container resizes; call this explicitly if you changed the container
size imperatively.
declarations.title: resizeChart

declarations.comments:
Clear the chart canvas. Used internally between animation frames;
rarely needed directly. Returns the chart instance for chaining.
declarations.title: clearChart

declarations.comments:
Look up the internal metadata object for a dataset by index. The
returned `Foreign` is Chart.js's `ChartMeta` — a mutable bag of
controller/scale/data references. Consumers who need specific fields
(e.g. `data`, `visible`, `hidden`) coerce via `Foreign` operations.
declarations.title: getDatasetMeta

declarations.comments:
Find chart elements matching an event for a given interaction mode.
Useful for click handlers that need to know which point/bar/arc was
hit. The `useFinalPosition` flag controls whether to use the
animation's final position (`true`) or current position (`false`).
declarations.title: getElementsAtEventForMode

declarations.comments:
Convert `InteractionOptions` to a plain JS object suitable for passing
to Chart.js's `getElementsAtEventForMode`. `Nothing` fields are dropped
so Chart.js uses its defaults for them. Exported for testing — callers
normally use `getElementsAtEventForMode` directly.
declarations.title: interactionOptionsToForeign

declarations.comments:
Toggle the visibility of a single data element by its index within
the dataset. Inverts the current visibility flag on the matching
meta entry. Call `updateChart` afterwards to render the change.
declarations.title: toggleDataVisibility

declarations.comments: Check whether a single data element is currently visible.
declarations.title: getDataVisibility

declarations.comments:
Get the array of currently active (hovered or otherwise highlighted)
chart elements. Each entry is an opaque `Foreign` wrapping Chart.js's
`ActiveElement` (which has `datasetIndex`, `index`, and `element`).
declarations.title: getActiveElements

declarations.comments:
Programmatically set the active elements (e.g. to highlight a data
point from outside a hover handler). Each entry must specify a
`datasetIndex` and `index`. Call `updateChart` to apply.
declarations.title: setActiveElements

declarations.comments:
Check whether a Chart.js plugin is currently enabled by its plugin id
(the string returned by the plugin's `id` field). Useful for
conditional behavior in custom plugins.
declarations.title: isPluginEnabled

declarations.comments:
Get the sorted list of visible dataset metadata objects. Each entry
is the same opaque `Foreign` shape returned by `getDatasetMeta`,
filtered to only the visible datasets and sorted in draw order.
declarations.title: getSortedVisibleDatasetMetas

declarations.comments: Return the number of currently visible datasets.
declarations.title: getVisibleDatasetCount

declarations.comments:
Register one or more Chart.js components (controllers, plugins,
scales, or elements) globally. Each item must be a JS object the
Chart.js registry recognizes — typically a controller class with an
`id` field. After registration, the component is available to all
subsequent chart instances in this process. Pair with `CustomType`
from `Chartjs.Types` (#27) to use a registered controller as a
chart type.

The PS argument is `Array Foreign` because Chart.js components are
arbitrary JS objects (and registering plugins / controllers / scales
/ elements works the same way at the API level). Build the
components in JS or via `unsafeToForeign` from PS.
declarations.title: chartRegister

declarations.comments:
Reverse `chartRegister`. Removes the given components from the
global registry. Mostly useful for tests; production code rarely
needs to unregister.
declarations.title: chartUnregister

declarations.comments:
Look up an existing chart instance by its canvas element. Returns
`Nothing` if no chart is currently bound to the canvas. Useful for
accessing a chart from outside the code that created it (e.g. from
a separate event handler that has only the DOM reference).
declarations.title: getChartByElement

declarations.comments:
Look up an existing chart instance by canvas element id (the `id`
attribute of the underlying `<canvas>`). Returns `Nothing` if no
matching canvas exists or no chart is bound to it.
declarations.title: getChartByCanvasId

declarations.comments:
Get the global Chart.js registry as an opaque `Foreign` value.
The registry exposes typed sub-registries (`controllers`, `plugins`,
`scales`, `elements`) plus generic `add` / `remove` methods. Use
this for advanced registration scenarios — registering a custom
interaction mode (via `Chart.helpers.registry`) or a custom tooltip
positioner, both of which the typed PureScript API does not yet
model. Consumers using this should reference Chart.js's registry
API docs directly.
declarations.title: chartRegistry

declarations.comments:
Check whether a pixel coordinate falls inside the chart area
(the plotting rectangle, excluding axes/legends/padding). Useful
for custom event handlers that need to distinguish clicks inside
the chart from clicks on surrounding chrome.
declarations.title: isPointInArea

declarations.comments:
Return the chart's context object — `{ chart, type }`. The
`chart` field is the Chart.js instance itself; `type` is a string
like `"chart"`. Primarily used inside custom plugins that need to
pass context down. Returned as `Foreign` because the shape is
self-referential.
declarations.title: getChartContext

declarations.comments:
Notify all registered plugins of a lifecycle hook. `hook` is the
event name (e.g. `"beforeDraw"`, `"afterUpdate"`). `args` is an
arbitrary JS object passed to each plugin's handler — build via
`unsafeToForeign`. Returns `false` if any plugin returned `false`
to cancel the hook, `true` otherwise. Rarely needed outside
custom plugin authoring.
declarations.title: notifyPlugins

modules.name: Chartjs.FFI

modules.comments:
Encoder machinery for Chart.js scriptable options (#20).

Mirrors `Chartjs.Colors` in spirit: takes `Scriptable` / `ScriptableIndexable`
values from `Chartjs.Types` and produces `Foreign` suitable for the overlay
merge in `Chartjs.FFI`. `SVal` / `SIVal` / `SIArray` round-trip through the
caller-supplied inner encoder (`a -> Foreign`); `SFn` / `SIFn` are wrapped
as JS closures that apply the inner encoder to the scriptable function's
return value at Chart.js's option-resolution time.

Consumed by `Chartjs.Colors` for dataset field overlay extraction
(#21 colors, #22 remaining properties).

declarations.comments:
Encode a `Scriptable a` to `Foreign`. Static `SVal` values pass through
the supplied encoder; `SFn` functions become JS closures that apply the
encoder to their return value at call time — which is what Chart.js
expects when it resolves scriptable options at draw time.
declarations.title: scriptableToForeign
scriptableIndexableToForeign

declarations.comments: `true` when the value needs the overlay path because it holds a function.
declarations.title: hasScriptable

declarations.comments: `true` when the value needs the overlay path because it holds a function.
declarations.title: hasScriptableIndexable

modules.name: Chartjs.Scriptable

modules.comments: PureScript types, enums, and default constructors for Chart.js configuration.

declarations.comments:
Supported Chart.js chart types. The 8 named constructors cover all
built-in types; `CustomType` is an escape hatch for chart types
registered at runtime via `Chart.register(MyController)`, where
`MyController` is a controller class (not a plugin) that defines a
new `id` such as `"financial"`, `"candlestick"`, or a custom
doughnut variant. The wrapped string in `CustomType` must match the
controller's `id` field so Chart.js can look it up at draw time.
Pairs naturally with roadmap item #25's registration FFI bindings,
but useful standalone for any consumer who has registered a
controller via raw JS.
declarations.title: ChartType

declarations.comments:
Convert to the Chart.js string representation. `CustomType` passes
the wrapped string through unchanged — Chart.js looks up the
corresponding registered controller at runtime.
declarations.title: chartTypeToString

declarations.comments: Element position within the chart.
declarations.title: Position

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: positionToString

declarations.comments: Text alignment options.
declarations.title: TextAlign

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: textAlignToString

declarations.comments: Chart axis identifier.
declarations.title: Axis

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: axisToString

declarations.comments:
Point marker styles. `PSFalse` disables the marker. `PSImage` wraps an
`HTMLImageElement` or `HTMLCanvasElement` for image-based point styles —
the referenced element's natural `width` / `height` determine the drawn
size at render time (the `pointRadius` field does not apply).

Image point styles cannot be JSON-serialized and go through the overlay
mechanism in `Chartjs.Colors` / `Chartjs.Callbacks` — you must create the
chart via `createChartWithCallbacks` (or anything that builds overlays)
for a `PSImage` dataset pointStyle to render. Ensure the wrapped image
has finished loading before the chart is created; Chart.js reads
`width`/`height` at draw time and draws nothing if they are zero.

Note: `PSImage` is currently supported only on `Dataset.pointStyle`.
Setting it on options-level fields (legend, element defaults) silently
produces a `null` in the JSON config with no overlay replacement.
declarations.title: PointStyle

declarations.comments:
Convert to the Chart.js string representation. Returns `""` for
`PSImage`, which has no string form — use `pointStyleToJson` and the
overlay path for serialization.
declarations.title: pointStyleToString

declarations.comments: Which border to skip on bar elements. `BSFalse` draws all borders.
declarations.title: BorderSkipped

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: borderSkippedToString

declarations.comments: Arc border alignment mode.
declarations.title: BorderAlign

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: borderAlignToString

declarations.comments: Canvas line cap style.
declarations.title: CapStyle

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: capStyleToString

declarations.comments: Canvas line join style.
declarations.title: JoinStyle

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: joinStyleToString

declarations.comments: Cubic interpolation mode for line charts.
declarations.title: CubicInterpolationMode

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: cubicInterpolationModeToString

declarations.comments: Interaction mode for hover and tooltip detection.
declarations.title: InteractionMode

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: interactionModeToString

declarations.comments: Scale types supported by Chart.js.
declarations.title: ScaleType

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: scaleTypeToString

declarations.comments: Time units for the time scale.
declarations.title: TimeUnit

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: timeUnitToString

declarations.comments: Animation easing functions.
declarations.title: Easing

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: easingToString

declarations.comments: Data decimation algorithms for large datasets.
declarations.title: DecimationAlgorithm

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: decimationAlgorithmToString

declarations.comments: When to draw the filler plugin relative to other chart elements.
declarations.title: DrawTime

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: drawTimeToString

declarations.comments: A value that Chart.js accepts as either a single value or per-data-point array.
declarations.title: Indexable

declarations.comments: Data point formats supported by Chart.js.
declarations.title: DataPoint

declarations.comments: Fill target for line/area charts.
declarations.title: Fill

declarations.comments: Stepped line interpolation.
declarations.title: Stepped

declarations.comments: Padding: uniform number or per-side.
declarations.title: Padding

declarations.comments: Animation can be a config object or `false` to disable.
declarations.title: AnimationSetting

declarations.comments: Scale display: true, false, or "auto".
declarations.title: ScaleDisplay

declarations.comments:
Scale `min` / `max` / `suggestedMin` / `suggestedMax` value. Numeric
for linear/logarithmic/radial scales, string for category scales
(matches a label) and time scales (an ISO date or any string the
configured time adapter parses). Chart.js's `CategoryScaleOptions`
and `TimeScaleOptions` both type these as `string | number`; the
base `CoreScaleOptions` types them as `unknown` (fully untyped).
declarations.title: ScaleMinMax

declarations.comments:
Context passed by Chart.js to scriptable option functions at
resolution time. Mirrors Chart.js's `ScriptableContext<TType>`
interface. `chart`, `dataset`, `parsed`, and `raw` are left as
`Foreign` because their shape varies by chart type and parsing
config — consumers inspect them via `Foreign` operations.
declarations.title: ScriptableContext

declarations.comments:
Context passed by Chart.js to scriptable tooltip option functions.
Mirrors Chart.js's `ScriptableTooltipContext<TType>`. Different
from dataset-level `ScriptableContext` — has `tooltip` and
`tooltipItems` instead of `dataIndex`, `dataset`, etc. Both
`tooltip` and `tooltipItems` are left as `Foreign` because their
shape is complex (the full `TooltipModel` / `TooltipItem` types).
declarations.title: ScriptableTooltipContext

declarations.comments:
Context passed by Chart.js to scriptable scale/tick/grid option
functions. Mirrors Chart.js's `ScriptableScaleContext`. Different
from dataset-level `ScriptableContext` — has `scale` and `tick`
fields instead of `dataset`, `parsed`, etc. `chart` and `scale`
are left as `Foreign` because their shape is complex; `tick` is
also `Foreign` (wraps `{ value :: Number, label? :: String |
String[], major? :: Boolean }`) — consumers inspect via `Foreign`
operations. Note: the `Scriptable` / `ScriptableIndexable` ADTs
are parameterised on `ScriptableContext` in the type, but at
runtime Chart.js passes this context shape for scale options.
declarations.title: ScriptableScaleContext

declarations.comments:
Context passed by Chart.js to scriptable point-label option
functions on radial scales. Mirrors Chart.js's
`ScriptableScalePointLabelContext`. Has `label` (the point label
string) instead of `tick`.
declarations.title: ScriptableScalePointLabelContext

declarations.comments:
A value that Chart.js accepts as either a static value or a
function computed from the resolution context. Chart.js calls
`Scriptable` functions once per affected element at draw time
to pick the concrete value. Use `SVal` for static values and
`SFn` (or the `scriptableFn` smart constructor) for dynamic ones.

`SFn`-wrapped functions travel through the overlay path: they
cannot be JSON-serialized, so the chart must be created via
`createChartWithCallbacks` or `createChartAuto` for the dynamic
form to take effect. `SVal`-only configurations serialize as
plain JSON and work with the base `createChart` path.
declarations.title: Scriptable

declarations.comments:
Chart.js's `ScriptableAndArray<T, TContext>` — a value that can be
a single value, a per-item array, or a scriptable function. Note
that Chart.js does **not** allow function entries inside the
array form, so this is a flat ADT rather than
`Indexable (Scriptable a)`.

Like `Scriptable`, the `SIFn` form cannot be JSON-serialized and
travels through the overlay path — the chart must be created via
`createChartAuto` (or `createChartWithCallbacks`) for an `SIFn`
value to take effect. `SIVal` / `SIArray` serialize as plain JSON
and work with the base `createChart` path.
declarations.title: ScriptableIndexable

declarations.comments:
Wrap a static value as a `Scriptable` field value (`Just (SVal a)`).
Mirrors the `single` / `perItem` convention — returns `Maybe` so the
result can be dropped directly into a `Maybe (Scriptable a)` record
field without a manual `Just`. For pattern matching use the raw
`SVal` constructor instead.
declarations.title: scriptableValue

declarations.comments:
Wrap a `ScriptableContext -> a` function as a `Scriptable` field value
(`Just (SFn f)`). Returns `Maybe` for record-literal ergonomics.
declarations.title: scriptableFn

declarations.comments:
`ScriptableIndexable` field value wrapping a single static value
(`Just (SIVal a)`). Returns `Maybe` for record-literal ergonomics.
declarations.title: siValue

declarations.comments:
`ScriptableIndexable` field value wrapping a per-item array
(`Just (SIArray xs)`). Returns `Maybe` for record-literal ergonomics.
declarations.title: siArray

declarations.comments:
`ScriptableIndexable` field value wrapping a `ScriptableContext -> a`
function (`Just (SIFn f)`). Returns `Maybe` for record-literal ergonomics.
declarations.title: siFn

declarations.comments: `spanGaps` accepts boolean or number (max gap size in axis units).
declarations.title: SpanGaps

declarations.comments: `clip` accepts number, per-side object, or false.
declarations.title: Clip

declarations.comments: `stacked` accepts boolean or "single" (stacks positive+negative together).
declarations.title: Stacked

declarations.comments: `cutout` accepts number (pixels) or string (percentage like "50%").
declarations.title: Cutout

declarations.comments: `barThickness` accepts number or "flex".
declarations.title: BarThickness

declarations.comments: `isoWeekday` accepts boolean or number (0-6 weekday index).
declarations.title: IsoWeekday

declarations.comments: Bar chart `borderWidth` as uniform number or per-side object.
declarations.title: BarBorderWidth

declarations.comments: Bar chart `borderRadius` as uniform number or per-corner object.
declarations.title: BarBorderRadius

declarations.comments: Arc `borderRadius` as uniform number or per-corner object.
declarations.title: ArcBorderRadius

declarations.comments:
Bar inflate amount: a pixel count or `'auto'` (0.33px when bars
are tightly packed, 0 otherwise). Chart.js default is `'auto'`.
declarations.title: InflateAmount

declarations.comments:
Point label display mode: boolean or `'auto'` (show labels only
when associated data is visible). Chart.js default is `true`.
declarations.title: PointLabelDisplay

declarations.comments: Multi-line label: single string or array of strings.
declarations.title: Label

declarations.comments: Title/subtitle text: single string or array of strings for multi-line.
declarations.title: TitleText

declarations.comments: A color value that Chart.js accepts: CSS string, gradient, or pattern.
declarations.title: Color

declarations.comments: Opaque type for a CanvasGradient object (from canvas 2D context).
declarations.title: CanvasGradient

declarations.comments: Opaque type for a CanvasPattern object (from canvas 2D context).
declarations.title: CanvasPattern

declarations.comments: Convenience constructor for CSS color strings.
declarations.title: css

declarations.comments:
Convenience constructor for `PSImage` from a typed `HTMLImageElement`.
Equivalent to `PSImage (unsafeToForeign img)`. For `HTMLCanvasElement`
or other `CanvasImageSource` values, use `PSImage` directly with
`unsafeToForeign`.

The referenced image must be fully loaded before the chart is created —
Chart.js reads `width`/`height` at draw time and draws nothing if they
are zero. Route the config through `createChartWithCallbacks` so the
overlay merge can substitute the live reference into the config.
declarations.title: psImage

declarations.comments: Font configuration for Chart.js text elements.
declarations.title: Font

declarations.comments:
CSS font-weight value. Mirrors Chart.js's `FontSpec.weight` which
accepts specific keyword strings or a numeric weight (100–900).
declarations.title: FontWeight

declarations.comments:
CSS line-height value. Mirrors Chart.js's `FontSpec.lineHeight` which
accepts a unitless number or any CSS string (e.g. `"1.5em"`, `"150%"`).
declarations.title: LineHeight

declarations.comments:
Row of dataset configuration fields shared between instances (`Dataset`)
and per-type defaults (`DatasetDefaults`). Parameterised by `r` so the row
can be extended with identity fields (label/data) to form `Dataset`, or
closed (with `()`) to form `DatasetDefaults`.
declarations.title: DatasetCommonRow

declarations.comments: Unified dataset configuration covering all Chart.js chart types.
declarations.title: Dataset

declarations.comments:
Dataset defaults — same shape as `Dataset` minus the identity fields
(`label`, `data`). Used as the value type of per-chart-type entries in
`DatasetsDefaults` (`options.datasets.line`, `options.datasets.bar`, ...).
declarations.title: DatasetDefaults

declarations.comments:
Per-chart-type dataset defaults (`options.datasets` in Chart.js). Each
entry supplies default field values for datasets of that chart type.
declarations.title: DatasetsDefaults

declarations.comments:
Outer radius for pie/doughnut charts. Same shape as `Cutout`: pixels or
percentage string (e.g. `"100%"`).
declarations.title: Radius

declarations.comments: Scale axis title configuration.
declarations.title: ScaleTitleConfig

declarations.comments: Scale tick mark configuration.
declarations.title: TicksConfig
GridConfig

declarations.comments:
Scale border line configuration.
#36: `dash`, `dashOffset` are now `Scriptable`.
declarations.title: ScaleBorderConfig

declarations.comments: Time scale configuration.
declarations.title: TimeConfig

declarations.comments:
Radial scale angle line configuration.
#40: color, lineWidth, borderDash, borderDashOffset now Scriptable.
declarations.title: AngleLinesConfig

declarations.comments:
Radial scale point label configuration.
#41: color, backdropColor, borderRadius now Scriptable; font Scriptable (#44).
#45: display accepts PointLabelDisplay (boolean or 'auto').
declarations.title: PointLabelsConfig

declarations.comments: Full scale axis configuration.
declarations.title: ScaleConfig

declarations.comments: Chart title (or subtitle) plugin configuration.
declarations.title: TitleConfig

declarations.comments: Legend label appearance configuration.
declarations.title: LegendLabelsConfig

declarations.comments: Legend title configuration.
declarations.title: LegendTitleConfig

declarations.comments: Chart legend plugin configuration.
declarations.title: LegendConfig

declarations.comments:
Tooltip plugin configuration (#35: all fields except `mode`,
`intersect`, and `boxPadding` are `Scriptable<T, ScriptableTooltipContext>`
in Chart.js TS). Color fields widened from `String` to `Color` so
consumers can use gradients or patterns as tooltip colors.
declarations.title: TooltipConfig

declarations.comments: Data decimation plugin configuration.
declarations.title: DecimationConfig

declarations.comments: Filler plugin configuration for area fills.
declarations.title: FillerConfig

declarations.comments:
Built-in `colors` plugin options. When `enabled` (default `true`), the
plugin auto-assigns colors to datasets from a built-in palette. Set
`forceOverride` to `true` to override any user-set colors.
declarations.title: ColorsPluginOptions

declarations.comments: Top-level plugin configuration.
declarations.title: PluginsConfig

declarations.comments: Animation timing and easing configuration (tier 1).
declarations.title: AnimationConfig

declarations.comments: Animation interpolation type.
declarations.title: AnimationType

declarations.comments: Convert to the Chart.js string representation.
declarations.title: animationTypeToString

declarations.comments: A value used as animation start/end point.
declarations.title: AnimationValue

declarations.comments: A per-property animation entry: enabled with config, or disabled (`false`).
declarations.title: AnimationPropertySetting

declarations.comments: Per-property animation config (used in tier 2 `options.animations`).
declarations.title: AnimationPropertyConfig

declarations.comments:
Per-property animation configuration (tier 2).
Keys are property names or group names (e.g., "colors", "numbers", "x", "tension").
declarations.title: AnimationsConfig

declarations.comments:
Animation overrides for a specific update mode (tier 3). The
`animation` field is `Maybe AnimationSetting` (not `AnimationConfig`)
so consumers can pass `Just AnimationDisabled` to fully disable a
per-mode animation — the canonical Chart.js idiom for "instant
hover transitions" or "no-animation resize."
declarations.title: TransitionConfig

declarations.comments:
Per-mode transition overrides (tier 3).
Keys are mode names: "active", "hide", "show", "reset", "resize", or custom.
declarations.title: TransitionsConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultAnimationPropertyConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultTransitionConfig

declarations.comments: Hover and tooltip interaction configuration.
declarations.title: InteractionConfig

declarations.comments:
Options passed to `getElementsAtEventForMode`. Matches Chart.js's
`InteractionOptions` interface — same fields as `InteractionConfig`
minus `mode` (which is passed as a separate argument to the method).
declarations.title: InteractionOptions

declarations.comments:
Single element returned from `getElementsAtEventForMode`. Matches
Chart.js's `InteractionItem`. `element` is the underlying Chart.js
element (point, bar, arc, etc.) — opaque, consumers inspect via
`Foreign` operations if needed.
declarations.title: InteractionItem

declarations.comments:
Chart layout configuration. `padding` is `Scriptable<Padding>` in
Chart.js TS — a scriptable function can compute padding based on
the chart context at render time.
declarations.title: LayoutConfig

declarations.comments:
Default styling for point elements.
All fields `ScriptableIndexable` per Chart.js `ScriptableAndArrayOptions`.
#43: added hoverBackgroundColor, hoverBorderColor, drawActiveElementsOnTop.
declarations.title: PointElementConfig

declarations.comments:
Default styling for line elements.
All fields `ScriptableIndexable`.
#43: added hover fields, spanGaps, segment.
declarations.title: LineElementConfig

declarations.comments:
Default styling for bar elements.
All fields `ScriptableIndexable`.
#43: added base, hover fields.
declarations.title: BarElementConfig

declarations.comments:
Default styling for arc elements (pie, doughnut, polar area).
All fields `ScriptableIndexable`.
#43: added hover fields, spacingMode.
declarations.title: ArcElementConfig

declarations.comments: Default element styling overrides.
declarations.title: ElementsConfig

declarations.comments: Top-level chart options.
declarations.title: ChartOptions

declarations.comments: Complete chart configuration: type, data, and options.
declarations.title: ChartConfig

declarations.comments: Default dataset with empty label and data. All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultDataset

declarations.comments:
Default `DatasetDefaults` with all optional fields set to `Nothing`. Use
as a starting point for per-chart-type defaults in `DatasetsDefaults`.
declarations.title: defaultDatasetDefaults

declarations.comments: Default `DatasetsDefaults` with every per-type entry set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultDatasetsDefaults

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultFont

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultScaleTitleConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultTicksConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultGridConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultScaleBorderConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultTimeConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultAngleLinesConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultPointLabelsConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultScaleConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultTitleConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultLegendLabelsConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultLegendTitleConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultLegendConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultTooltipConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultDecimationConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultFillerConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultColorsPluginOptions

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultPluginsConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultAnimationConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultInteractionConfig

declarations.comments:
All optional fields set to `Nothing`. Default options for
`getElementsAtEventForMode` — Chart.js will use its built-in defaults
for any field left `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultInteractionOptions

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultLayoutConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultPointElementConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultLineElementConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultBarElementConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultArcElementConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultElementsConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultOptions

declarations.comments: Default chart config with `Bar` type, empty labels, and empty datasets.
declarations.title: defaultConfig

declarations.comments:
Custom parsing keys for mapping data object properties to axes.
Use `xAxisKey`/`yAxisKey` for cartesian charts, `key` for radial charts (pie/doughnut/radar).
declarations.title: ParsingConfig

declarations.comments: All optional fields set to `Nothing`.
declarations.title: defaultParsingConfig

declarations.comments: Wrap a single value as an indexable option.
declarations.title: single

declarations.comments: Wrap per-item values as an indexable option.
declarations.title: perItem

declarations.comments: Convert plain numbers to DataPoint values.
declarations.title: fromNumbers

declarations.comments: Convert (x, y) tuples to XY data points.
declarations.title: fromXY

declarations.comments: Convert (label, value) tuples to XYString data points (for time/category x-axis).
declarations.title: fromXYString

declarations.comments: Convert {x, y, r} records to bubble data points.
declarations.title: fromBubble

declarations.comments: Convert (min, max) tuples to floating bar data points (range bars).
declarations.title: fromFloatingBar

declarations.comments: Create uniform padding.
declarations.title: uniformPadding

modules.name: Chartjs.Types
